observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant create and maintain a special account under the general fund for the recording of income and expenses, as well as assets, liabilities and equity accounts pertaining to the economic enterprises of the LGU.  At the end of the year, the financial statements be prepared for each special account in the General Fund.
We recommended that the accountable officers observe daily remittance of collections as provided in the above Circular to avoid possible risk of loss or misuse.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management take full opportunity on the application of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and fully comply with the prescribed guidelines and procedures, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, the disposition of non-existing/missing PPE items, the procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability, and the recording of accounting entries and disclosures in the notes to financial statements, for the purpose of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with concerned national government agencies, facilitate the registration and transfer of titles of the subject parcels of land including those where there are existing municipal buildings and other structures to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the LGU.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management:

a) Prepare and submit the PIF as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets, property and interests of the LGU with the GIF of the GSIS; and

b) Ensure that the identified assets, property and interests are insured with the GIF of the GSIS to protect the LGU against the risks of non-indemnification in case of damage to or loss of property.

We recommended that Management:

a) Determine and identify, based on available records and thru confirmation with various agencies, the source agencies of the subject unutilized balances; and

b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to process immediately the return of the unutilized balances of fund transfers to concerned source agencies and/or to the National Treasury, as applicable.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that Management direct the MENRO-Designate to properly utilize the MRF which has been found not operational for over a year, and closely monitor the proper maintenance of the MRF.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management adhere with the imposition of warranty securities in the form of retention money or special bank guarantee for the delivered and accepted goods, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those project beneficiaries and other third parties involved in the execution of government projects.

We recommended that Management thru the Municipal Engineer:

a) Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project that the appropriate project signboards are already posted; and

b) Provide complete information as to the starting date of the project, target date of completion and project status in the project signboards to promote transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare and maintain RAAOs per function/program/project, allotment class, current/continuing appropriations and fund for proper monitoring of balances of available appropriations and allotments to avoid the incurrence of obligations in excess of available allotments/appropriations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer strictly require from the supplier of goods the issuance of sales invoice as principal evidence, and attach the same to the disbursement vouchers for submission to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly present prior period errors and adjustments by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance with the requirements under Paragraphs 46 to 54 of IPSAS 3.

We recommended the following:

a) . . .

b) The Local Development Council, if possible, include in its AIP, specific and detailed programs, projects and activities, such that all that the LCE needs to do is to execute the contract, and no further approval or authorization by the sangguniang bayan is required; and

c) . . .

We recommended that Management:

a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to create and maintain a special account in the general fund for the receipt and utilization of the BGCM with complete and separate subsidiary ledgers, trial balance and financial statements; and

b) . . .

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer undertake periodic reconciliation of balances of RPT and SET Receivable accounts per books of accounts with that of the balances per master list of RPT delinquencies to come up with accurate and reconciled RPT and SET Receivable account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Effect the necessary adjustments in the LGU’s books of accounts to reflect the actual cost of Merchandise Inventory pertaining to the remaining lots held for sale, and the actual cost of Investment Property, Land pertaining to lots held for investment purpose; and
Trace and verify documents relative to the installment payments for the subject subdivided lot which were previously recognized as unearned income and recorded as Other Deferred Credits and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the full payment made for the said lot. Copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) be submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Compute for the annual depreciation of the Public Market and Market Lights by preparing a lapsing schedule, taking into consideration the cost of these assets, year and month of completion and useful life, and record annually the appropriate depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation of these assets;
Effect the necessary adjustments for the recognition of the CY 2023 depreciation expenses and the corresponding accumulated depreciation of the Public Market and Market Lights; and
Reclassify the cost and the correct accumulated depreciation of Market Lights into Other Infrastructure Assets and Accumulated Depreciation – Other Infrastructure Assets, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to record in the books of accounts the subject parcels of land at fair/market value and provide disclosures for the same in the notes to financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the donation of motor vehicles to concerned barangays for the fair presentation of the PPE and expense accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant conduct analysis of the Accounts Payable, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the subject accounts payable into its appropriate liability accounts;
The Municipal Accountant carefully analyze transactions/payables, properly record the payables pertaining to the receipt of goods or services on account in the normal course of trade and business operation as accounts payable, and record other payables into their appropriate liability accounts, taking into consideration the description of accounts in the Revised Chart of Accounts; and
The Management cause the reversion of accounts which remained outstanding for over two (2) years and for which no actual claim has been filed, or which is not covered by perfected contracts on record, or for which the purpose of the trust fund has already been accomplished.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the noted variances between the balances per books and per bank, and record the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers to support the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account;
Provide complete and proper disclosures in the Notes to FS pertaining to the amounts and details of the unexpended balances of the DRRMF for the current and prior years; and
Examine the cash deficit and/or discrepancies between the reported Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account and the corresponding Cash in Bank – LCCA, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly present prior period errors and adjustments by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance with the requirements under Paragraphs 46 to 54 of IPSAS 3.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant carefully review and record the transfer of current year’s unspent/unexpended balances of LDRRMF to the STF using the “Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds” account in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant create and maintain special accounts under the general fund for the recording of income and expenses, as well as assets, liabilities and equity accounts pertaining to the economic enterprises of the LGU.  At the end of the year, the financial statements be prepared for each special account in the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fully observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the Notes to Financial Statements including, but not limited to, the disclosure for the reconciliation of net cash flows from operating activities to surplus/(deficit) and reconciliation of comparison of budget and actual amounts and the statement of financial performance.

We recommended that Management:
Make representations with concerned source agencies, determine the necessity of maintaining the subject cash in bank accounts with unused/idle funds and return such funds to the source agencies and/or revert the same to the National Treasury or the unappropriated surplus in the general fund, if warranted, to be made available for appropriation to support other priority government programs, projects and activities;
Exert efforts to retrieve available documents such as plans and program of works relative to the abandoned Irrigation Project and/or make representations with DAR and LBP for any available documents, and consider the take-over of the remaining works by administration or contract in accordance with Section 53.3 of the 2016 revised IRR of RA No. 9184, taking into consideration the available/remaining funds for the said project; and
Conduct analysis on the outstanding balance of the depository account under the Trust Fund for the BIR eFPS, remit immediately to the BIR any existing obligations/taxes withheld, and/or revert the same to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund, if warranted, and process the closure of this account.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for each local road;
The General Services Officer prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each local road; and
The Municipal Accountant present in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosure for local road networks.

We recommended that:
The Management determine and identify, based on available records and thru confirmation with various agencies, the source agencies of the subject unutilized balances;
The Municipal Accountant process immediately the return of the unutilized balances of fund transfers to concerned source agencies and/or to the National Treasury, as applicable; and
The Municipal Accountant exert effort to trace prior years’ records including financial statements, trial balances, ledgers and/or supporting schedules to determine cause of the abnormal/negative balances, and effect the necessary adjustment, as warranted.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the LDRRM Council to ensure that only disaster risk reduction and management activities are included in its LDRRMF Investment Plan and the annual budget for the 5 per cent LDRRMF. In relation thereto, funding for administrative expenses, Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) and Capital Outlay (CO) requirements of the LDRRM Office be included in the appropriations of said office under the Office of the Municipal Mayor; and
Refrain from charging expenditures against LDRRMF which are not directly related to the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management activities namely, disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended that Management, thru the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant, carefully review and monitor the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF and ensure that only the unexpended balances of the QRF and MF-MOOE are transferred to the STF, while unexpended balances of the MF-CO be made continuing in the General Fund books until the projects funded therefore are completed and any savings thereof be made available for use in other disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer properly monitor the disbursements relative to the LDRRMF based on the actual source of funds, that is General Fund or Special Trust Fund, to avoid erroneous charges of expenditures.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management, in close coordination with the PSA, establish, maintain and institutionalize a CBMS as a tool for the formulation and implementation of poverty alleviation and development programs.

We recommended that Management correct the noted deficiencies, taking into consideration the recommended actions/measures from the BFP personnel, and comply with the fire safety measures prescribed under Rule 10, Chapter 2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9514 to ensure the safety of the occupants of government buildings and structures and to prevent the risks of damage or loss of life and property.

We recommended that Management exert effort to collect the amount paid to the contractor by demanding the immediate refund of the same, or collect from the surety bond posted by the contractor, or from any money due or which may become due the contractor.

We recommended that:
The Bids and Awards Committee perform its function in determining the eligibility of prospective bidders in the procurement of goods/services conducted thru alternative methods of procurement by requiring the submission of eligibility documents/requirements such as, but not limited to, Mayor’s/Business Permit and PhilGEPS Certificate of Registration or PhilGEPS Registration Number, as applicable; and
The Municipal Accountant carefully review disbursements for goods procured thru alternative methods of procurement and ensure that the eligibility documents/requirements of suppliers are attached before claims are processed.

We recommended that Management:
Completely fill out the details in the Pre- and Post-Repair Inspection Report, such as, but not limited to, the serial/engine no., acquisition date and cost of the equipment, date of last repair and nature of last repair;
Completely fill out the details in the WMR, such as the date of the report and the signature of the witness to the actual disposition; and
Direct the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain a report/document indicating the repair history of equipment and motor vehicles for Management to have a proper assessment of its repair and maintenance requirements, and attach such report/document in the disbursement vouchers for the payment of these expenditures.

We recommended that Management, through its GFPS, endorse to the SB, the passing of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313, which if passed, be disseminated or posted in conspicuous places. Further, include in the ordinance the following: (a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; (b) imposition of fines/penalties for violation thereof; and (c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.
We recommended that Management create/assign a separate RC and RC Code for the GFPS to account, monitor and report GAD expenses and other GAD-related financial transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost and related accumulated depreciation of the public market as Investment Property, Building and Accumulated Depreciation - Investment Property, Building for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that:

a) The Municipal Accountant:

i. . .; and

ii. . .

b) The Municipal Treasurer, acting as General Services Officer:

i. Prepare the annual Report of Local Road Network; and

ii. . .

c) The Inventory Committee prepare the RPCLRN with complete details as to road network ID, road components, construction dates and conditions of each road network; and

d) The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Inventory Committee reconcile the balances of their respective accounting records, property records and the result of the physical count relative to road networks.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a) . . .; and

b) Present the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic account in the Statement of Financial Position and the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments to recognize cost of the purchased engine as an addition to the carrying amount of the subject Transportation Equipment. Further, we recommended the derecognition of the carrying amount of the replaced defective component/part of the vehicle in accordance with the derecognition provisions in IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as applicable, and carefully review and record financial transactions using the appropriate accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer coordinate in the preparation of an accurate and complete SCBAA, taking into consideration the original budgeted amounts in the annual budget, the amounts of supplemental budgets, if any, and the balances of all continuing appropriations. Also, the Municipal Accountant present proper disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for any changes/material differences between the original and final budget.

We recommended the following:

a) The Municipal Budget Officer refrain from approving obligations in excess of and/or without appropriation for certain object of expenditures. Otherwise, legal sanctions shall be imposed for its continued incurrence; 

b) The Management, thru its LDRRM Council, conduct proper planning and identification of PPAs in its LDRRMF Investment Plan, and proper allocation of appropriations for each identified PPA; and

c) The LDRRM Officer closely monitor the expenditures necessary for the implementation of PPAs identified under its LDRRMF Investment Plan and ensure that the PPAs implemented are those included/identified in the said plan. Otherwise, the LDRRM Council initiate the preparation of supplemental investment program for any realignment of funds affecting the LDRRMF and submit the same to the sangguniang bayan for approval.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council, thru its LDRRM Officer:

a) Ensure the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen the LGU’s disaster risk reduction and management activities; and

b) Identify and incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded from the previous years' unexpended balances of LDRRMF which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and incorporate the same in the AIP for approval by the sangguniang bayan.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer the subject unexpended balances of the LDRRMF to the STF and recognize the same as “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund books of LGU. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant observe annually the transfer of unexpended balances of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the STF to be used for LDRRM programs, projects and activities within the next five years in accordance with the LDRRMF Investment Plan.

We recommended that Management:

a) Direct the Municipal Engineer to conduct analysis on the type of repair and/or spare parts needed for the repair of the subject construction/heavy equipment, including the costs of repair and spare parts thereof. If cost of repairs is found to be excessive or not economical, Management to conduct comparative analysis of the cost of repairs needed as against the purchase of new heavy equipment;

b) . . .; and

c) Make representations with the Provincial Government of Zamboanga del Sur for the release/return of the confiscated construction/heavy equipment.

We recommended that the Municipal Economic Enterprise Development Officer (MEEDO) improve the operations of the waterworks system, and devise measures/strategies, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to improve the collection efficiency of waterworks system fees.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management, thru its Bids and Awards Committee, prepare its APP using the form prescribed by the GPPB to cover all procurements including those items in the APP-CSE; revise and update the APP as needed, and submit the same to the GPPB and the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed deadlines. Henceforth, no procurement shall be undertaken without an approved APP.

We further recommended that the BAC prepare its PMR using the form prescribed by the GPPB to include all procurement activities undertaken thru competitive bidding and other alternative modes of procurement, as well as the APCPI Results, and submit these reports to the GPPB within the prescribed deadlines. Further, copies of these reports be furnished the Office of the Auditor for monitoring and as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

We recommended that Management, thru its BAC, adopt competitive bidding as the general method of procurement and resort to Direct Contracting and/or other alternative methods of procurement if and when the conditions are met as provided under Rule XVI of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Bids and Awards Committee post the Notice of Award, Purchase Order, including Notice to Proceed, if necessary, in the PhilGEPS website, the LGU website, if available, and at any conspicuous places in the premises of the LGU, of goods and services undertaken thru alternative methods of procurements with ABC of above P50,000.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a) Submit immediately the periodic reports and transaction documents relative to the receipts and disbursements of government funds which were not yet submitted to facilitate the audit team’s timely audit and verification of accounts and transactions; 

b) Devise a tool/mechanism to eliminate the cause of delay in the submission of periodic reports and transaction documents and effectively speed up the processing, segregation and compilation of pertinent documents; and

c) Strictly observe the timely submission of periodic reports and transaction documents within the reglementary period.

We recommended that Management:

a) Review its organizational structure, the functions of each office/department, and its procedures in the preparation of contracts/purchase orders and the subsequent submission of copies of the same to the Office of the Auditor, which should be coursed thru the Municipal Treasurer, acting as the General Services Officer; and

b) Direct the BAC Secretariat to expedite its preparation and compilation of procurement and bidding documents, and forward the same to the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation and execution of contracts/purchase orders.

 Further, we reiterated our recommendations that Management:

a) Furnish the Office of the Auditor with copies of contracts/purchase orders relative to the procurement of goods and services, including the relevant supporting documents, within five (5) days after execution to facilitate timely review and evaluation of the same; and

b) Submit notice of deliveries to the Office of the Auditor relative to procured items within the prescribed period to facilitate the conduct of inspection.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly compute the withholding taxes from payments to suppliers using the appropriate tax rates, taking into consideration whether they are VAT or Non-VAT Registered, to avoid over/under withholding of taxes.

We recommended that Management, thru its GAD Focal Point System, update its GAD database to include data and other gender-information relating to the institutional sector to better identify organization-focused gender issues and concerns. Further, we recommended that Management include sourcing of data and GAD related information from local offices of NGAs to have a more comprehensive GAD Database. In compliance thereto, Management may consider the allocation of budget in its GAD Plan and Budget to utilize available resources for the collection and processing of data requirements of the GAD Database.

We recommended that Management include in its GAD Plan and Budget the attendance of the members of the GAD Focal Point System to capability trainings/seminars to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender analysis and gender audit through the use of various gender assessment tools. Henceforth, the GFPS institutionalize the conduct of gender analysis and gender audit through the use of gender assessment tools.

We recommended the following:

a)	The Management create an Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of local road networks and to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network not later than January 31 of each year;

b)	The Municipal Accountant present in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosure for local road networks, and prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for each local road; and

c)	The Municipal Treasurer acting as the General Services Officer prepare the annual Report of Local Road Network, and prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each local road.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to identify the discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile the same, and effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted, to reflect the correct account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:


a)	Prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers to support the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account;


b)	Provide complete and proper disclosures in the Notes to FS pertaining to the amounts and details of the unexpended balances of the DRRMF for the current and prior years; and

c)	Examine the cash deficit and/or discrepancies between the reported Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account and the corresponding Cash in Bank – LCCA, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly present prior period errors and adjustments by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance with the requirements under Paragraphs 46 to 54 of IPSAS 3.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fully observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the Notes to Financial Statements including, but not limited to, the disclosure on carrying amounts for each class of PPE, reconciliation of net cash flows from operating activities to surplus/(deficit), and reconciliation of comparison of budget and actual amounts and the statement of financial performance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant create and maintain a special account under the general fund for the recording of income and expenses, as well as assets, liabilities and equity accounts pertaining to the economic enterprises of the LGU.  At the end of the year, the financial statements shall be prepared for each special account in the General Fund.
We recommended that Collectors remit their collections daily or frequently as possible to avoid risks of loss and misuse.

We recommended that Management make representation with source agencies, determine the necessity and authority of maintaining the non-moving cash in bank accounts and revert the same to the National Treasury or the unappropriated surplus in the general fund, if warranted, to be made available for appropriation to support other government programs and projects especially those for the response to COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended that Management take full opportunity on the application of the prescribed guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, if any, for the purpose of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU.

We further recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare the Physical Inventory Plan, to be approved by the local chief executive and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period, and to comply with the prescribed unique property number indicated in the property stickers used for inventory tagging.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer, acting as the General Services Officer, to facilitate the registration and transfer of titles of the land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Direct the Property Unit to prepare the IIRUP for all unserviceable property, and the WMR for all waste materials, subject for disposal;

b)	Create a committee responsible for the appraisal of unserviceable property and supplies, whose appraisal shall be considered as the floor price in the case of disposal thru sale at public auction; and

c)	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to dispose of the unserviceable property and supplies in accordance with the rules and regulations on supply and property management in local government units.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Prepare and submit the Property Inventory Form as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets, property and interests of the LGU with the GIF of the GSIS; and

b)	Ensure that the identified assets, property and interests are insured with the GIF of the GSIS to protect the LGU against the risks of non-indemnification in case of damage to or loss of property.

We recommended that Management optimize the utilization and implementation of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning of project including timing of execution; coordinate action of LGU officials for better result; and strict monitoring of project implementation to ensure timely accomplishment of the project. We recommend further that the MPDC in coordination with the Municipal Development Council (MDC) ensure that the identified projects to be included in the AIP for the 20% Development Fund are properly selected, planned and evaluated.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Effect the full utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen the LGU’s disaster preparedness; and

b)	Direct the LDRRMO together with the members of the LDRRMC to identify and incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded from the previous years' unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended that Management direct the LDRRMO/LDRRMC to prepare a supplemental investment program for any realignment of funds affecting the LDRRMF or for any changes made thereto for COVID-related PPAs and to submit the same to the local sanggunian for approval.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management prepare its APP to cover all procurements including those items in the APP-CSE; revise and update the APP as needed, and submit the same to the Government Procurement Policy Board and the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed deadlines. Henceforth, no procurement shall be undertaken without an approved APP.

We recommended that Management compute for liquidated damages arising from the delays incurred by contractors in the completion of infrastructure projects, and deduct/collect the same from the retention money, other securities posted by the contractor, or from any money due or which may become due the contractors, whichever is available to the Management.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer determine and resolve the causes of delay in the submission of periodic reports and transaction documents relative to the receipts and disbursements of government funds, and strictly observe the timely submission of the same within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

b)	Submit notice of deliveries to the Office of the Auditor relative to procured items within the prescribed period to facilitate the conduct of inspection.

b) . . .

We recommended  that the Municipal Accountant shall issue a certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books before a new cash advance is processed and approved as required under existing rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advance to ensure efficient and effective control of the cash advance.
observations.reason: There are still cash advances granted without the Accountant’s Certification, however, it had already been liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Property Officer/ Munici-pal Treasurer to submit copies of Perfected Contracts or Purchase Orders and the necessary attachments therein to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period to ensure a systematic and effective review process to determine its price reasonableness and the propriety, validity and legality of the contractual agreements. Also, the required docu-ments as enumerated and indicated above be submitted to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and stop paying claims without complete supporting documents and that vouchers onwards be duly processed and properly documented before they will be approved and paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts and avoid possible suspension and/or dis-allowance in audit.

We hereby recommend to the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Records and the Munici-pal Accountant to record deliveries of supplies or Property in the books of accounts of the agency. The latter to maintain Supplies Ledger Card per stock number, PPE Ledger Card for each category of assets, and Real Property Ledger Card for Land.
observations.reason: There are still process of evaluating the system to incorporat the inventories

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should make an inventory of the unserviceable properties and initiate action for its disposal should be determined to be no longer needed, or beyond economic repair the earliest possible time to avoid further deterioration.
observations.reason: Municipal Engineer informed that unserviceable properties were all heavy equipment and that they find difficulty in transporting same to the designated place near the LGU as the subject equipment were no longer functioning.

observations.recommendation: We advise that Cashbook balances should be reconciled with the Subsidiary Ledgers maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant once a month or quarterly as required by existing regulations so that any discrepancy/ies noted in the process can be acted/corrected upon immediately.
observations.reason: Reconcilia tion is  delayed  due to different cut-off date between the AO and the Accounting

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor through her MSWDO should immediately implement and utilize the budget of the program of the National Agency upon receipt of the money intended for the purpose otherwise, any unused funds by the partners should be returned to the DSWD upon completion of the program as provided in Administra-tive Order No. 08, s. 2012 on the Omnibus Guidelines in the Implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program.

We require the Municipal Treasurer to include in the application for fidelity bond the various accountable officers of the Municipality whose function is to collect taxes and other revenues. This is advantageous on the part of the municipal government in case of loss of government funds through theft, fire or other force majeure. And, refrain from allowing revenue collectors to perform the collection function if he is not duly bonded.

We recommend that responsible officials be required to prepare and submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund on or before the 15th day of each month to the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.  To facilitate the preparation of the reports, the required subsidiary records be maintained.
observations.reason: Agency submitted but very much delayed.

observations.recommendation: We advise that management: (1) ensure that meals and snacks served during staff meetings or monthly conferences and for entertainment of visitors/guests shall be properly charged against the representation allowance of the concerned LGU officials; (2) require the Municipal Accountant to check the propriety and validity of the claims; and (3) request the Municipal Budget Officer to properly charge the transactions within the limitations of the budget.

We recommended  Management  allocate and appropriate funds for the conversion of its open dumpsite to controlled dumpsite and likewise activate its Solid Waste Management Board to help address the problem on wastes and guaranteed compliance with Republic Act No. 9003. Moreover,  concerned citizens or households also be responsible for their own wastes and have it segregated to help attain a clean environment.
observations.reason: The agency already allocated funds and designated a person to manage the garbage disposal but still they are looking for a site.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Accountant submit the reports and documents immediately to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the municipal government. Unjustifiable failure on the part of the official or employee concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of his salary, until she shall have complied with the aforesaid requirement, without prejudice to the action that may be instituted against her (Sec. 122, P.D. 1445).
observations.reason: There were still delayed in their submission of disbursement vouchers and other reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials comply with Sections 352 and 315 of RA 7160 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-83 to attain the objectives of the law and issuances.
It is recommended that the concerned officials/offices should see to it that disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documentation as required in existing regulation.

We recommended  that the Honorable Municipal Mayor limit the issuance of Travel Order to those that are related to ones’ work assignments and functions taking into consideration the austerity measures that the government is taking and the necessity of the expenses.
We recommended that management to provide a garage or motor pool for their heavy equipment and vehicles, or find an area to protect these properties from natural elements, theft and misuse.
observations.reason: Management accepted their lapses and justified that some of the heavy equipments are located on site.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) shall revisit and reconcile the approved Annual Investment Plan to ensure that the projects/programs/activities are in accordance with the guidelines set and reconcile the AIP with the Accomplishment Report. The Municipal Budget Officer, on the other hand shall approve the Obligation Request (ObR) in accordance with the guidelines set under Paragraph 3.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1, dated April 13, 2011. Further, we are reminding the Local Chief Executive that utilizing such fund, whether willfully or through negligence, for any purpose beyond those expressly prescribed by law or public policy shall be subject to the sanctions provided under the Local Government Code and under such applicable laws. The 20% Development Fund shall be appropriated and disbursed only for the very purpose for which such fund was established.
observations.reason: The concerned officials admitted their lapses and promised to fully implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: The designated Municipal Accountant should submit the reports and documents immediately to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the municipal government. Unjustifiable failure on the part of the official or employee concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of her salary, until she shall have complied with the aforesaid requirement, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against her (Section 122, P.D. 1445).
observations.reason: There are still DVs in the SEF that were not submitted on time due to absence of monitoring of submission.

observations.recommendation: Require the collectors/designated collectors to incorporate all the accountable forms issued to them in the Report of Collections and Deposits upon remittance. Require the Municipal Treasurer to demand the presentation of all unused accountable forms issued to the accountable officers, together with the collections made during each remittance.

We recommend that the collections for the day should be deposited/remitted intact daily to the Municipal Treasurer. On the other hand, the Municipal Treasurer shall see to it that every time collections are turned over to him, all accountable forms whether issued or not must be presented and accounted for. To ensure strict adherence to rules and regulations, appropriate disciplinary action shall be taken against accountable officers who fail to perform their functions and to comply with existing rules and regulations.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer (MT) accepted his lapses that same was not given attention and promised to implement the recommendation. The MT has issued Memo-randum dated Dec. 20, 2015 to all collectors to regularly liquidate their collections.

observations.recommendation: This is a prior year’s audit finding. We reiterate the previous recommendation for the accountable officers concerned to liquidate fully their cash advances so that proper accounting could be made thereof to ensure fairness of accounts presentation in the financial statements. The Municipal Accountant as an internal auditor should strictly implement the rules and regulations of cash advances to avoid accumulation of huge amount of unliquidated cash advances by – Sending written notices or a reminders to the concerned accountable officers to comply within ten (10) days before the expiration of the 30 or 60 days period specified under Sec. 14 of Executive Order No. 248 and other applicable issuances by enjoining them to liquidate their outstanding cash advances; Instituting appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees, by withholding  or suspension of their salaries; and Complying and implementing Items 6.0 and 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 with subject: “Demand for Immediate Liquidation and Settlement of All Cash Advances Outstanding as of December 31, 2011.
observations.reason: Cash advances were still granted even when the previous cash advances were not yet liquidated. However, substantial amount of the balance had been liquidated.

observations.recommendation: An inventory committee should be created to conduct the annual physical inventory of all assets to ensure fairness of accounts presentation in the financial statements.  In the preparation of the report, the prescribed format should be used and the different columns should be properly filled-up to ensure that all items are properly identified and accounted for. Assignment of property number should be properly done so that each of the items can be easily identified and properly classified. On the other hand, the Accounting Unit shall prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards; Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card and Real Property Ledger Cards.  Once the physical inventory of PPE is already available it shall be reconciled with the (a)  PPE ledger card, (b)  the control accounts and (c)  property cards. If any discrepancies exist,  it  should  be  immediately  verified   and  adjusted.  Further,  reclassification of obsolete and unserviceable assets, as well as assets no longer used by LGU-Guipos to “Other Assets” account should be made.
observations.reason: A Committee was already created but they are still in the process of doing the physical count. However, management identified list of equipment which are unserviceable.

observations.recommendation: Management shall (1) ensure that meals and snacks served during staff meetings or monthly conferences and for entertainment of visitors/guests shall be properly charged against the representation allowance of the concerned LGU officials and for (2) require the Municipal Accountant to check the propriety and validity of the claims; and (3) the Municipal Budget Officer to properly charge the transactions within the limitations of the budget.
observations.reason: Concerned officials accepted their lapses, and promised to correct the same.
